subject: English
title: If I Were You – Hard Short Notes

key_points: The play presents a psychological conflict between Gerrard and the intruder
The intruder believes he can escape justice by stealing Gerrard’s identity
Gerrard manipulates the intruder through clever dialogue and reasoning
The turning point occurs when Gerrard traps the intruder in the cupboard
The play ends with justice restored and the criminal captured
characters: Gerrard – Represents intelligence, courage and presence of mind
The Intruder – Represents crime, violence and overconfidence
themes: Crime and overconfidence lead to failure
Calm thinking is essential in crisis
literary_devices: Irony – Gerrard’s polite tone hides his strategic intentions
Symbolism – The revolver represents danger and tension
exam_tips: Discuss the psychological battle between Gerrard and the intruder
Explain the symbolic meaning of the revolver and setting
Analyse the theme of intelligence versus violence
Highlight how dialogue drives the dramatic tension
